Canine Liberation
nytimes_articles.abstract:
Cheers for the urban guerrillas of New York City, the dauntless men and women who let their dogs loose in the parks! Willing to pay $100 fines and even go to  jail, these individuals are demanding that parts of Central Park become leash-free. Dog lovers of New York, unite! You have nothing to lose but choke chains, and the parks to win. 

     In effect, these heroes are saying that the time has come for New York and other American cities to unleash their dogs. That's certainly the practice in Boston and Cambridge, Mass., where certain parks, as well as Harvard Yard, are regularly enjoyed by unleashed dogs.

nytimes_articles.abstract:
The ancient gods and goddesses, in white Greek garb, are arrayed on stacked wooden cubes with Zeus and Hera at the top. Zeus descends, sounding strikingly like Elvis as he sings: 

     I use lots of thunderbolts.

nytimes_articles.headline: A Right to Be Safe
nytimes_articles.abstract:
How many widows are there in the former Yugoslavia? No one has thought to tally them. Of the 250,000 war dead, most were men and boys between the ages of  15 and 50. Countless men are missing -- perhaps 8,000 from Srebrenica alone. 

     Wives have become heads of households. Some nurture hopes that somewhere, somehow, their husbands and children who were in concentration camps are alive.  In the meantime, they need help to rebuild their lives.
